title: PagerDuty incident-response performance metrics
description: One record per SERVICE over a bounded look-back window: the service's opaque id, the window bounds, the incident / acknowledged / resolved counts, and incident-response time AGGREGATES — time-to-acknowledge (MTTA) and time-to-resolve (MTTR), each as a mean plus p50 / p90 / p95 in whole seconds. Derived read-only from the PagerDuty REST API (incidents endpoint, since/until window). AGGREGATE-ONLY — the grain is the service, NEVER a named responder; the connector never decodes WHICH individual acknowledged or resolved an incident. The over-collection boundary is structural: the connector's record types have no field that can hold a responder identity (name / email / id / contact), and a reflection guard fails the build if one is added. Proves incidents are acknowledged and resolved within target windows at the PROGRAM level (SOC 2 CC7.4 'responds to identified security incidents'; the incident-handling + continuous-monitoring concepts SCF labels IRO-02 + MON-02, here mapped to the present catalog anchors IRO-09 Incident Reporting + MON-01 Continuous Monitoring) — a program-level posture, NOT a per-engineer scorecard. The connector reports a descriptive timing posture; the platform evaluator owns the final pass/fail per (control, scope). x-default-scf-anchors are default mapping hints flagged for maintainer recheck (OQ #9); the spec candidates IRO-02 + MON-02 are absent from the bundled SCF catalog fixture, so IRO-09 + MON-01 — the closest present anchors — are used (see slice 654 decisions-log D2).

service_id.description: PagerDuty-native stable identifier of the service this aggregate is for (an opaque ID, not a credential). The aggregation grain — never a responder.

window_start.description: Inclusive start of the look-back window the aggregates cover (RFC 3339).

window_end.description: Inclusive end of the look-back window the aggregates cover (RFC 3339).

incident_count.description: Total number of incidents observed for the service in the window. The denominator that gives the aggregates meaning.

acknowledged_count.description: Number of those incidents acknowledged at least once (the MTTA sample size).

resolved_count.description: Number of those incidents resolved in the window (the MTTR sample size).

mtta_seconds.description: Time-to-acknowledge aggregates (whole seconds) over acknowledged_count incidents. Zero across the board when acknowledged_count is 0.

mttr_seconds.description: Time-to-resolve aggregates (whole seconds) over resolved_count incidents. Zero across the board when resolved_count is 0.
